title: VP of Software Engineering
description:
VP of Software Engineering
Canada, Remote
Apply

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This role is for a high-impact engineering leader who has been in the trenches at fast-scaling startups — someone who has grown from individual contributor to leader and helped scale engineering orgs through multiple phases of growth. Ideally, you've done it more than once.

As VP of Software Engineering, you'll be responsible for building and maintaining a high-performing, scalable engineering organization across Product Development, Ops, and Technical Support. This is a role for someone who’s hands-on enough to spot an issue i

Electrical Designers 

We’re growing our Design & Consulting team and looking for talented Electrical Designers to help shape the future of shipbuilding!

From powering innovative vessels to solving real-world engineering challenges, your work will directly support the creation of world-class ships.

 

Whether you’re launching your career or bringing years of expertise, you’ll collaborate with a passionate team, work with cutting-edge tools, and play a hands-on role in bringing ambitious designs to life with accuracy, creativity, and impact.

Piping Designers

 

We're seeking skilled and detail-oriented Piping Designers, of all levels, to join our Design & Consulting team in supporting innovative shipbuilding and marine engineering projects!

 

Our team is split into two main divisions that complement each other, 3D modeling and Manufacturing information (MI). The 3D model team takes engineering documents and turns them into a fully integrated digital ship. The MI team takes the 3D model and produces 2D drawings that the shipyard production facilities can fabricate from.

 

In this role, you’ll play a key part in developing piping systems and layouts for new vessel construction and refit programs. Working closely with other disciplines, production teams, and regulatory bodies, you’ll help bring complex designs to life using advanced 3D modeling tools and industry software to produce 2D drawings.

At Genoa, founded in 1995 and a leader in production design and 3D modeling services for the shipbuilding and offshore industries, we believe success is built by people who are passionate, curious, and committed to excellence.

title: Chief Financial Officer
description:
For more than 60 years, Northwood has been a powerful advocate for seniors and a leader in continuing care across Nova Scotia. Established as a social movement grounded in dignity, belonging, and choice, Northwood has grown into Atlantic Canada’s largest not for profit continuing care organization, serving more than 7,000 people through long-term care, home care, retirement living, and innovative community-based programs.

Northwood has been recognized for leading change in continuing care, advancing innovative models, embracing technology, and prioritizing dignity, choice, and belonging. Built on values of accountability, social justice, respect, and continuous improvement, Northwood remains deeply focused on people and ensuring individuals and families receive high-quality, compassionate care.

The Opportunity

Northwood is seeking a strategic, experienced, and mission-aligned Chief Financial Officer (CFO) to provide executive financial leadership within a large, complex, multi-site continuing care organization. Reporting to the President and CEO, the CFO is a key member of the senior leadership team and plays a central role in ensuring Northwood’s long-term financial sustainability while supporting high-quality, person- and family-centred care.

The CFO will partner closely with executive colleagues, operational leaders, and the Board of Governors to ensure sound financial stewardship, informed decision-making, and organizational resilience.

Yorkville University is actively seeking its next Chair, Faculty of Education, to provide academic leadership and operational oversight for programs within the Faculty, including the Master of Education in Educational Leadership. This is a compelling opportunity to lead nationally relevant programs, influence academic quality and growth, and contribute meaningfully to a university committed to access, flexibility, and student success.

Yorkville University
At Yorkville University and Toronto Film School, we believe education is more than the pursuit of knowledge – it is a catalyst for transformation. Our mission, grounded in democratizing education, is to create access for learners from every background and empower them to achieve their aspirations and advance their careers.

We are committed to delivering rigorous, flexible programs that are both personally fulfilling and professionally rewarding, while contributing to the betterment of society. Since 2004, Yorkville has been among Canada’s few private, for-profit, fully accredited degree-granting universities, with campuses in Ontario, British Columbia, and New Brunswick. title: General Manager
description:
Ashburn Golf Club is one of Atlantic Canada’s premier private clubs, combining over a century of tradition with a modern and engaging member experience across Halifax and Fall River. Established in 1922 and designed by renowned Canadian architect Stanley Thompson, Ashburn is known for its championship-calibre golf and strong, community-oriented culture.

With two courses in Halifax and Fall River, along with dining, events, and year-round programming, the Club offers a dynamic and well-rounded experience supported by an engaged membership and a strong governance model.

As the Club enters an exciting period of growth, Ashburn Golf Club is seeking a strategic and engaging General Manager (GM) to lead the organization through this next phase and ensure continued operational excellence and long-term success.

What does this role entail?

Reporting to the Board of Directors, you will be responsible for setting and executing the Club’s strategic direction, with full accountability for financial performance, operations, and member experience. You will lead all core functions, including golf operations, food and beverage, facilities, membership services, and administration, while fostering strong stakeholder relationships and operational excellence.

You will work closely with the Board, Trustees, and Committees to support effective governance and decision-making, leading key capital and operational initiatives while building a high-performing team culture rooted in accountability,

title: President
description:
Sault College is actively seeking an innovative and people-centred President to lead the College into its next chapter, guided by a new strategic plan. The President will guide a dedicated team in fostering an inclusive, student centred culture while navigating a rapidly evolving post secondary landscape. This is a pivotal opportunity to strengthen academic excellence, deepen community partnerships, and position Sault College as a resilient and trusted anchor institution for Sault Ste. Marie and Northern Ontario.

Sault College
Sault College has a proud history characterized by tremendous growth and spanning over more than five decades, evolving from a Vocational Centre opening in 1965 to the College it is today. Each year, the College responds to the postsecondary educational needs of more than 4,000 part-time and full-time students and continues to add new programs across all disciplines. Sault College currently offers 57 programs including 16 College Certificates; 29 Ontario College Diplomas; 4 Ontario College Advanced Diplomas; 6 Ontario Graduate Certificates; and 2 Bachelor’s Degrees. The Sault College campus is home to a diverse array of new and upgraded educational facilities including the Waterfront Adventure Centre, Motive Power Centre, and iE3.

title: Director, Fire Services
location: West Hants Regional Municipality, Nova Scotia, CA
description:
West Hants Regional Municipality is seeking an accomplished fire services professional and people-focused leader to serve as its next Director of Fire Services.

West Hants Regional Municipality

West Hants Regional Municipality (WHRM) is a dynamic and growing community in Hants County, Nova Scotia, formed through the amalgamation of the former Town of Windsor and the Municipality of the District of West Hants in 2020. Strategically situated along the Avon and St. Croix Rivers, the region offers a compelling blend of small-town charm and regional connectivity, with easy access to Halifax and the broader Atlantic Canadian economy. Home to a population of just over 19,500, WHRM combines the close-knit feel of its key communities, including Windsor and Hantsport, with the space and character of its surrounding rural landscapes.
West Hants Regional Municipality is a mix of 52 rural and urban communities. It is the first region to welcome you as you arrive in the Annapolis Valley from Halifax.  With more than 1,250 square kms, there is something in every nook and cranny to inspire you to explore more and linger longer. West Hants borders on Kings County to the northwest and Halifax County to the south.

The Role 

Under the direction of the Chief Administrative Officer, the Director of Fire Services is responsible for the oversight of all fire services that support the Municipality and would collaborate with and support protective services such as Ground Search and Rescue (GSAR),

Humber Polytechnic
Located in Toronto, one of the most diverse cities in the world, Humber Polytechnic is a leading Canadian post-secondary institution recognized for its innovative, polytechnic approach to education. As an Institute of Technology and Advanced Learning and a founding member of Polytechnics Canada, Humber serves more than 76,000 full-time and part-time learners across six Faculties and four campuses. The institution offers an extensive range of credentials, from apprenticeships and diplomas to bachelor’s and master’s degrees, all grounded in experiential, career-focused learning that prepares graduates to thrive in a rapidly evolving workforce.

For over 50 years, Humber has delivered a rich academic and student experience rooted in teaching excellence, applied research, and strong industry connections. Its award-winning faculty bring deep academic expertise and real-world experience into state-of-the-art labs and classrooms that mirror professional environments. Through robust partnerships with industry, community organizations, and global institutions, Humber ensures students gain meaningful work-integrated learning opportunities and access to influential networks.

title: Medicare Advisor
description: About UsPropel builds technology that strengthens the social safety net in America. We envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.The safety net’s strength depends not just on funding, but delivery. Propel exists to dramatically improve how government benefits are delivered. Managing SNAP benefits, picking a D-SNP plan, or protecting against EBT theft should feel as seamless as Apple Pay or Netflix.Governments struggle to build modern, scalable user experiences. We don’t.Over 5.5 million Americans trust Propel today. We’ve grown revenue 35% YoY and are reinventing entire benefit categories, including Medicare, around the real needs of low-income Americans. We’re building a high-growth, high-ceiling, sustainable for-profit company where impact drives revenue.About the RolePropel serves approximately 1M users who are Medicare eligible, most of whom are also Medicaid eligible. We are building the first-ever app-based brokerage focused on helping "duals" (Americans on Medicare and Medicaid) enroll in healthcare plans. We specialize in Dual Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs) and are expanding to 30 states in 2026, with plans to move into larger Medicare markets over the next 24 months. As a Medicare Advisor, you will be a trusted expert, helping users compare Medicare plans, enroll with confidence, and maximize their healthcare benefits. You will also collaborate with our EPD (engineering, product, design) team to refine how we suppor

title: Senior Solutions Architect, Government Partnerships
description: About UsPropel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.About the RoleWe're looking for a Solutions Architect to bridge Propel's Government Partnerships team and our Product & Engineering organization - translating state partners' needs into product strategy, and then owning the delivery of that work with states. This role has the opportunity to shape the way our product evolves with deeper government partnerships and increase the value we’re able to deliver to both users and government partners.This is a hands-on IC role. You'll be the person writing the scoping doc, building the dashboard, sitting in the state meeting, configuring the campaign, and running the evaluation. You own both strategy and execution end to end. Over time, as this work scales, the role has potential to grow into a leadership position — but today we need someone who is excited about the execution.You'll work across the state partnership funnel, from early relationship-building through campaign delivery. You should be the kind of person who can walk into a meeting with a state SNAP agency, understand their operational pain points around payment error rates or portal access, walk out with a concrete plan that maps to Propel's product roadmap — and then go execute it. The role reports into our Government Partnerships team,

title: Founding Sales Lead
description:
Home
Careers
Contact
View All Openings
Founding Sales Lead
St. John’s, NL (Hybrid)

Help build the commercial engine of an AI-powered safety company with real product, real customers, and industrial pilots deploying this summer.

Audyse is building intelligent PPE that restores clear communication and real-time situational awareness in the world's loudest, most dangerous workplaces. We've shipped product to 16 countries, we're well-funded, and we're deploying to industrial pilot sites this summer across oil & gas, marine, manufacturing, and heavy industry.

We're looking for a Founding Sales Lead to help us build our sales motion from the ground up. This isn't cold-calling from a script, it's figuring out how to sell a category-defining product into industries where the buyers are safety managers, operations leads, and EHS directors.


‍

What You’ll Do

This is not a traditional BDR role, it’s a chance to help build the commercial engine of a deep-tech startup.

You will:

Lead sales outreach, qualification, and discovery with enterprise and mid-market industrial customers.
Run customer discovery to deeply understand problems, workflows, and buying processes.
Help shape our storytelling, messaging, and positioning based on real customer insights.
Work with the founding team to build and refine landing pages, funnels, and experiments to test what resonates.
Drive top-of-funnel pipeline, schedule demos, and support enterprise sales cycles.
Collaborate with marketing to grow ou

title: Founding Firmware Developer
description:
Home
Careers
Contact
View All Openings
Founding Firmware Developer
St. John’s, NL (Hybrid)

First firmware hire at an AI-powered safety company with a major oil & gas contract and industrial pilots launching this summer.

Audyse is building intelligent PPE that restores clear communication and real-time situational awareness in the world's loudest, most dangerous workplaces. Our headsets suppress harmful noise while preserving speech, alarms, and environmental cues, paired with a software platform that gives safety teams live exposure data and actionable insights.

We've already shipped 400 consumer headsets to 16 countries, completed 4 hardware revisions in 12 months, and navigated international manufacturing from Newfoundland. We've secured a major R&D contract and have 5 industrial pilots committed for this summer.

As our first dedicated firmware developer, you won't just write code, you'll own the firmware architecture, make critical decisions about reliability, connectivity, and power management, and ship software that performs in industrial conditions where failure isn't an option. You'll work directly with our CEO and CTO.



‍AI-Native Development

We're an AI-native team. You'll use Claude Code, Cursor, and Copilot daily to accelerate development, generating drivers, test harnesses, utilities, and boilerplate, then critically review and harden that code for production embedded systems. AI isn't a replacement for deep embedded engineering judgment. It's a multiplier.

title: Founding Hardware Developer
description:
Home
Careers

Founding Hardware Developer
St. John’s, NL (Hybrid)

First hardware hire at an AI-powered safety company with a major oil & gas contract and industrial pilots launching this summer.

Audyse is building intelligent PPE that restores clear communication and real-time situational awareness in the world's loudest, most dangerous workplaces. Our headsets suppress harmful noise while preserving speech, alarms, and environmental cues, paired with a software platform that gives safety teams live exposure data and actionable insights.

We've already shipped 400 consumer headsets to 16 countries, completed 4 hardware revisions in 12 months, and navigated international manufacturing from Newfoundland. We've secured a major R&D contract and have 5 industrial pilots committed for this summer.

As our first dedicated hardware developer, you'll own the hardware stack. You'll design boards, bring them up in the lab, validate them in the field, and ensure they perform reliably on offshore rigs, factory floors, and vessel decks.
‍


What You'll Do

As a Founding Hardware Developer at Audyse, you will:

Design and iterate on schematics and PCB layouts for compact, industrial-grade IoT headset.
Conduct lab bring-up, debug boards, and validate hardware functionality.
Build test setups and collect data to inform hardware and firmware design decisions.
Collaborate with firmware, software, and product teams to ensure seamless integration.
Participate in field t

About the Department
Jobs, Growth and Rural Development

The Department of Jobs, Growth and Rural Development has a mandate to ensure the province has skilled workers for a fast-growing economy. This includes creating a climate which is conducive to economic growth, competitiveness and prosperity. This will be achieved through a spectrum of programs, services, and supports.

Property Paralegal
Property Paralegal | Charlottetown, PE



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

We are seeking an experienced Property Paralegal to join our Charlottetown office. The ideal candidate will bring a strong background in residential real estate transactions, excellent organizational skills, and the ability to manage files in a fast-paced environment. While we value collaboration and ongoing development, this role is best suited for someone who can confidently support property transactions with minimal supervision.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES


Working as part of a collaborative team, you will:

Assist lawyers with all aspects of residential property files;
Draft documentation for property transactions;
Act and a liaison between the firm and clients, providing clear and timely communication on the status of transactions;
Manage ordering title searches and liaising with external partners (banks, other law firms, Realtors, IRAC and others);
SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

Minimum two years of experienc

Senior Corporate Paralegal
Senior Corporate Paralegal | Charlottetown, PE



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

This is a senior role suited for a confident professional who can manage corporate files with minimal supervision and provide guidance and support to lawyers and junior team members.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES


Working as part of a collaborative team, you will:

provide comprehensive support to the firm’s clients in all aspects of corporate transactions, including asset and share purchases and sales, reorganizations, and financings;
independently complete incorporations, amendments, continuances, amalgamations, dissolutions, revivals, and reorganizations of Provincial and Federal entities;
conduct and coordinate due diligence reviews;
prepare and file PPSA registrations and related searches;
review, organize, and maintain complex corporate minute books;
assist lawyers with transactional closings and post-closing matters;
record billable and non-billable time in accordance with firm standards;

title: AI Software Engineer
description:
Be a part of our success story. Launch offers talented and motivated people the opportunity to do the best work of their lives in a dynamic and growing company. Through competitive salaries, outstanding benefits, internal advancement opportunities, and recognized community involvement, you will have the chance to create a career you can be proud of. Your new trajectory starts here at Launch!
Launch is actively seeking qualified, energetic engineers with passion for building solutions leveraging new and emerging technologies related to AI. This is a software engineering role specializing in applications with use cases powered by AI solutions, especially Generative AI, such as LLM integration, vector embeddings, real-time inference, and semi-automated, human-in-the-loop workflows. This role offers an exciting opportunity to be at the forefront of AI technology, working on diverse projects that drive real-world impact. If you’re passionate about AI and have the technical expertise to back it up, this role may be perfect for you!
Responsibilities Include:

Write high-quality, maintainable code in languages such as Python, JavaScript, C#, or others relevant to AI development
Work closely with and in cross-functional teams including software engineers, project managers, designers, QA, data engineers, and data scientists
Integrate with a variety of different APIs, services, and technologies to bring pre-trained models and other technologies to bear, such as cloud-based vector databa

title: Solution Architect
description:

Through competitive salaries, outstanding benefits, internal advancement opportunities, and recognized community involvement, you will have the chance to create a career you can be proud of. Your new trajectory starts here at Launch!
Launch is seeking an experienced Solution Architect to leverage both technical expertise and business insight to design, propose, defend, and deliver robust solutions for our clients. This individual will collaborate with cross-functional teams to turn cutting-edge technologies into impactful solutions. Problem Solving: Address complex business and technical challenges related to new technology implementations or the customization of existing systems.

Through competitive salaries, outstanding benefits, internal advancement opportunities, and recognized community involvement, you will have the chance to create a career you can be proud of. This role focuses on regulated industries such as healthcare and finance, requiring a deep understanding of compliance, security, and operational resilience.

Requisition ID: 1550 

Location: Port aux Basques, NL 

Hourly Assessment Rate: $19.097 

Hourly Rate 1: $30.598 (First 1440 hours of compensated service after completion of assessment period)

Hourly Rated : $34.771   

Application Closing Date : Until filled

 

Marine Atlantic is dedicated to creating an environment which promotes employee growth, career development, and job satisfaction.  Our employees embody our core values; they are diverse, safety-oriented team-players who exhibit integrity and commitment, and always strive for excellence.

 

Marine Atlantic is currently accepting applications for Bilingual Ticket and Reservation Clerks to work at our Terminal in Port aux Basques, NL. title: Saint John Litigation Lawyer
description: Are you an experienced litigator with a passion for law and a desire to contribute to diverse, impactful legal matters? Do you want to be part of a dynamic and growing firm that values both personal and professional development?McInnes Cooper is currently seeking an experienced Litigation Lawyer to join our thriving team in Saint John, New Brunswick, where you will have the opportunity to engage in a broad range of complex litigation matters. Our litigation team provides highly diversified services to clients across various industries, regularly appearing in courts at all levels in the Atlantic Provinces, as well as in the Federal Court and the Supreme Court of Canada. Additionally, the team frequently represents clients in administrative and regulatory tribunals, arbitrations, and mediations.As a Litigation Associate, your key responsibilities will include:Managing your own files while collaborating with colleagues on complex legal matters.Providing legal services across a wide array of areas, such as general/civil litigation, commercial litigation, estate litigation, insurance defense, and construction/engineering litigation.Conducting legal research, providing legal opinions, and maintaining up-to-date knowledge on relevant legal developments.Communicating effectively with clients, colleagues, and opposing counsel to ensure successful outcomes.Representing clients in court, at hearings, and in negotiations.The successful candidate will possess the following qualifications:

title: Senior Advisor, Government Relations (Federal) / Conseiller·ère sénior·e, Relations gouvernementales (niveau fédéral)
description:
Do you want to be part of a mission driven organization shaping Canada’s innovation future? Do you thrive in collaborative environments where creativity, continuous improvement, and strategic thinking are valued? Are you motivated by work that strengthens Canada’s competitiveness and delivers meaningful impact for communities and businesses? 

At Mitacs, we take a talent first approach to building Canada’s innovation capacity. We connect researchers with organizations across every region of the country to help solve pressing challenges and accelerate economic growth. By enabling business academic collaboration and supporting the development of highly skilled talent, we contribute to a resilient, future ready Canadian economy. 
 
Guided by our values — Impact, Integrity, Collaboration, Excellence, and Talent — we work with partners nationwide to advance research, commercialization, and innovation. 
 
At Mitacs, you become part of a team committed to empowering Canadian talent, fostering meaningful partnerships, and driving innovation that supports Canada’s long-term competitiveness. If you’re inspired by big ideas and passionate about creating national impact, we encourage you to apply. 

Senior Advisor, Government Relations (Federal, Contract until December, 2026)
The Senior Advisor will serve as the lead for government relations at the Federal level. The Senior Advisor will build and maintain relationships with federal government officials, policymakers, and key stakeholders

*This role is full time temporary with an end date of March 31, 2027.
 
Do you want to be part of our innovative and entrepreneurial team?
Mitacs is undergoing a major enterprise transformation to become a more client-centric, digitally enabled organization. This effort includes a wide-reaching digital modernization of our business applications, along with the convergence of data, process, and platforms to better deliver value to our stakeholders.
 
As part of this transformation, we are building a modern and resilient quality assurance practice—one that ensures reliability, usability, and performance across our systems and services. The QA Analyst plays a key role in this mission by helping evolve our testing strategy, implementing scalable automation, and ensuring product excellence through hands-on collaboration with developers, product managers, and business stakeholders.

You will report to the Enterprise Application Manager and play a vital role in shaping the tools and practices that support Mitacs’ transformation journey.

description: About UsPropel is on a mission to build technology that strengthens the social safety net. We’re a passionate team of ~100 Propellers who envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.The RoleAre you a Platform Engineer looking to make a big impact on a mission-driven company? Do you enjoy architecting software that shapes an entire engineering organization? Do you thrive in high-autonomy startup environments?We’re looking for a self-motivated Senior Platform Engineer to join our Developer Enablement Team within the engineering org at Propel.This role empowers other engineers by improving the cloud native platforms that enable development and delivery of Propel’s technology. These cloud native platforms impact several products and stacks, including interacting with government programs, surfacing savings opportunities, enrolling users in healthcare plans, internal tooling, architecture, and more! Your focus will be on running fault tolerant applications in Kubernetes and AWS, and solving organization-wide engineering problems. We are looking for a tinkerer to experiment with improvements to the developer experience at Propel. title: Senior Data Scientist
description:
Be a part of our success story. At Launch, we offer talented and driven professionals the opportunity to deliver high-impact work in a dynamic, fast-growing environment. We invest in our people through competitive compensation, exceptional benefits, internal growth pathways, and meaningful community involvement. For senior professionals ready to shape the future of AI and Data Science, your next big opportunity starts here.

The Role:
As a Senior Data Scientist, you will lead the design and development of advanced machine learning and AI solutions that solve complex business challenges. You’ll apply a deep technical foundation in data science, spanning statistics, modeling, software engineering, and domain expertise, while helping set technical direction, mentor team members, and influence decision-making across the organization. You will work closely with stakeholders and cross-functional teams to define use cases, architect scalable solutions, and drive innovation through GenAI, LLMs, NLP, computer vision, and other cutting-edge techniques.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead advanced Data Science initiatives from ideation to deployment, ensuring alignment with business strategy and measurable outcomes.
Develop, implement, and optimize AI/ML models across a range of domains, including predictive analytics, GenAI, LLMs, time series, deep learning, and NLP.
Conduct rigorous exploratory data analysis and feature engineering to uncover insights and support robust model development.

ABOUT US
 
RESOURCES

SENIOR PROGRAMMER

Do you dream of having a say in the software you write? Strong Data Automation is an old company with a start up mentality that currently specializes in waste management logistics. That’s garbage you say? Exactly! We are a tight team working to build essential, usable products for our customers quickly and everyone pitches in to get the job done right. You’ll work on problems of secure data collection, operation scaling and automation, and hardware integrations across multiple applications and geographical locations. Customers will love you for making their job about high level decision making instead of copy and pasting between spreadsheets. We handle tracking, billing, trending and more as we try to revolutionize how organizations control what happens to everything the world doesn’t want.

Computer science degree or equivalent experience / education

Familiar with modern development best practices and design patterns
 
Knowledge of key security fundamentals for web application development
 
Strong knowledge of SQL Server database
 
Thrives in an atmosphere of taking on new challenges and developing new skills

title: General Manager
description: Venor is proud to partner with Terra Nova Landscaping in their search for a General Manager to join their team in Bedford, Nova Scotia. Terra Nova Landscaping is a growing landscaping and construction services company known for its hands-on approach, strong industry relationships, and commitment to delivering high-quality work across the Halifax Regional Municipality. Supporting a variety of landscaping, construction, and site-related projects, the company has built a strong reputation through reliable execution and long-standing client partnerships. The General Manager will oversee day-to-day operations while supporting business development and project execution across the region. Reporting directly to ownership, this role is ideal for a hands-on leader with strong industry connections, operational experience, and the ability to help drive continued growth across the business. What You'll Be Doing In this role, you will lead operations across field teams, projects, and business development initiatives while ensuring projects are delivered safely, efficiently, and profitably. Key responsibilities include: Oversee daily operations, ensuring efficiency, productivity, and profitability across crews, projects, and equipment operations. Lead and manage field teams, equipment operators, supervisors, and project staff while fostering a strong culture of accountability and teamwork. Plan, coordinate, and deliver landscaping and construction-related projects on time and within budget.

The design team plays a significant role at trophi.ai. We shape how players first understand and feel the product, from the very first minute in the game through the loops that bring them back. We turn ambiguity into clear, intuitive experiences, use research and data to guide decisions, and partner closely with product, engineering, and data science to ship quickly. As a senior designer you will set the quality bar, own our systems and patterns, and define the look and feel of an AI powered coach that meets players inside their favorite games and our studio customers.

Things you’ll do

Lead end-to-end product design from discovery and flows to polished prototypes and user testing.
Turn ambiguous ideas into cohesive UX that drives core business outcomes around activation, retention and onboarding (First session clarity, engagement loops etc)
Partner with product and data science to run lightweight usability testing, interpret activation and retention funnels, and use insights to iterate quickly.
Obsess about impact, first on onboarding, first session clarity, and engagement loops.
Build and own our design system for speed and consistency across surfaces
Work with product, engineering, and data science to ship high q

title: Partnership Strategy Manager
description:
What we’re doingtrophi.ai is the AI powered coaching platform that transforms how gamers learn, compete, and win. Backed by a16z, with $12M in funding, we’re already live in five titles with 100K+ users and rapidly expanding into new games, hardware partnerships, and SDK integrations.
Our next stage of growth is about building the right ecosystem around trophi.ai from studios and publishers to hardware makers and platforms. We’re looking for a Partnerships Lead reporting to the head of product & partnerships who can hunt, structure, and close deals that unlock distribution, revenue, and product opportunities.

Things you’ll do

Build and own a pipeline of studios and publishers for our B2B product line focused on personalized onboarding.
Contribute to our ecosystem and market entry strategy, mapping opportunities and frameworks with the Head of Product.
Hunt and close new ecosystem partnerships (hardware, platforms, service providers) that generate revenue and elevate the player experience.
Manage and expand existing strategic partnerships (e.g., Fanatec, Corsair, Grid & Go), ensuring smooth delivery, reporting, and upsell opportunities.
Assist in Scoping, pitch, and deliver proof of concepts (POCs) with partners, then convert them into scaled commercial agreements.
Develop and deliver proposals, presentations, and partner materials that resonate with decision-makers.
Collaborate with Product and Data Science to scope integrations and deliver on partner requirements.
Represen

title: Strategy & Operations Associate
description:
What we’re doingWe’re building the AI-powered coaching platform that transforms how gamers learn, compete, and win. Backed by a16z with $12M raised, we’re live in 5 titles with 100K+ users and rapidly expanding into new games, hardware partnerships, and SDK integrations.

We’re hiring a Business Operations Associate to join our core team and work directly with the founders to build the backbone of our business. This is a unique opportunity to help scale the world’s first AI coaching platform for gamers at the intersection of gaming and AI.

What You’ll Do

Strategy & Analysis

Partner with the Head of Product & CEO on core strategic initiatives (B2B + B2C).
Lead market analysis, competitive deep dives, and opportunity sizing to identify trends, customer needs, and emerging opportunities.
Build business cases and quantitative models that inform go/no-go decisions on new games, partnerships, and investment bets.
Help chase down ambiguous, zero-to-one problems , from hypothesis to framework to action plan.

Ops & Execution

Build the operating backbone: architect workflows, OKRs, and business review processes that scale with the company.
Drive cross-functional initiatives across Product, Partnerships, and Marketing to ensure alignment and delivery.
Create leverage for leadership by taking point on pitch decks, proposals, and high-stakes projects that require polish and speed.

Growth Enablement

Support partnerships and BD with market research, TAMs, and strategic storytelling.

You’ll Thrive Here If…


You’re energized by ambiguity and move fast to bring clarity where others stall.
You get a rush from finding the simple, elegant solution to a messy, complex problem.
You think like a GM//always asking how a product choice impacts revenue, growth, and user success.
You’re creative at problem solving — able to structure messy problems, build frameworks, and beat ambiguity with clear, actionable paths forward.

title: Intermediate Product Designer
location: St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
description:
What we’re doing
We’re building the AI powered coaching platform that transforms how gamers learn, compete, and win. Backed by a16z, we’re already live in 5 titles with 100K+ users and rapidly expanding into new games and beyond our own app to power coaching for major partners in fitness, wearables, AR/VR, and even aviation. 

The design team plays a significant role at trophi.ai. We don't just build our own interface, we design "plug and play" coaching experiences that live inside our partners' ecosystems.

As a Product Designer, you will work hands-on to turn ambiguity into clear, intuitive experiences. You will partner closely with product and engineering teams to ship features quickly. Instead of managing others, your focus will be on craft: execute high-quality flows that might live in a mobile app, a post-session report, or an overlay in a simulator and designs that help users master complex skills, from landing a plane to perfecting a golf swing.

Things you’ll do

Execute end-to-end design and take projects from requirements and rough sketches to polished prototypes, embeddable UI components and SDKs.
Create cohesive UX flows that drive core business outcomes, specifically focusing on activation, onboarding, and first-session clarity.
Collaborate on research andsupport product and data science teams in running usability tests, interpreting funnels, and using those insights to iterate on your designs.

Careers at Virtual Marine
WHO ARE WE?

Virtual Marine is a privately held Canadian company with its headquarters in Paradise, Newfoundland and Labrador.  Our mission is to improve the safety of life at sea by providing realistic, effective, and safe training tools for small craft operators. Our simulators allow crews of small marine craft to train for real-life situations in environments that are often too dangerous and costly for live boat exercises. 

We’re growing our strong, cohesive, aligned team with a can-do attitude, that’s driven by our values. We foster an inclusive culture and it shows - staff turnover is lower than industry average. We celebrate wins, and take care of each other. 

WHAT DO WE OFFER?

We offer a competitive salary and benefits package and a rewarding challenge with our dynamic and growing team solving big problems!

Interested in seeing some of our work? 
Visit our YouTube channel @VMTechnology or check out the rest of our website or social media.

Project Manager (new) 
The Position

As the Project Manager, you will play a lead role in planning, coordinating and overseeing the successful implementation of client-based projects and deliverables. title: Account Executive, ERP Solutions US Midwest Region
description:
Account Executive, ERP – Midwest US 
Permanent Full Time Location: Remote - Midwest US (open to applicants in the following states: AL, AR, LO, MO, MS, TX)
Grow Your Earnings. Close Lucrative Deals. Build Your Career.
If you’re a motivated Account Executive who wants your earnings to reflect your performance, Vision33 has an opportunity for you to build a highly rewarding sales career.
 
At Vision33, we are looking for a driven Account Executive in Midwest, US, who is motivated by building relationships, closing net-new business, and significantly growing their income through performance. 

High performers thrive here. With our uncapped commission potential, strong sales support, and a proven delivery organization, Account Executives can substantially and consistently grow their income year over year.


The Role
As an Account Executive, you will take ownership of the full sales cycle for net-new ERP software opportunities, developing and executing strategies that generate pipeline, win new clients, and exceed revenue targets.
You will:

Drive net-new revenue growth by developing and managing a strong pipeline of qualified opportunities.
Prospect and engage new clients, building relationships with decision-makers from operations leaders to C-suite executives.
Forecast demand and manage your pipeline to consistently exceed monthly and annual sales targets.
Position Vision33’s full suite of solutions and services, ensuring prospects clearly understand the value of partnering wit

title: Solution Sales Engineer / Pre-Sales Solutions Consultant, SAP Cloud ERP (Public edition)
description:
Solution Sales Engineer / Pre-Sales Solutions Consultant, SAP Cloud ERP (Public edition – only)Full Time / Permanent Location: Open Location   - Canada
Travel Required: Throughout North America based on client needs. 
We’re in the process of expanding our dedicated SAP Cloud ERP (Public Edition) team to shape the future of ERP transformation. This high-impact team will play a critical role in how Vision33 delivers modern, cloud-first solutions that drive measurable value for our clients.
   
This is an exciting opportunity to join at the ground level and help define what success looks like. As Vision33’s newest Solutions Engineer, you’ll have the ability to make an immediate impact, influence how we grow, and build your career alongside a rapidly expanding cloud practice. This role also includes performance-based incentives tied to key business objectives.

As part of this strategic team, you’ll partner closely with both sales and delivery teams — helping shape solution strategy during pre-sales engagements, leading compelling demonstrations, and supporting successful customer outcomes. You’ll work alongside experienced leaders in a collaborative, fast-moving environment where storytelling, innovation, and a cloud-first mindset are highly valued.

We’re looking for professionals who thrive in modern Cloud ERP environments – those sales-minded, analytical, storytellers who bring curiosity, confidence, and the ability to lead conversations around transformation. Direct SAP Clou

title: Consultant en implantation / Senior Implementation Consultant, Sage Intacct (bilingue)
description:
Consultant en implantation de Sage Intacct (bilingue) Temps plein, permanent Lieu: Canada  Vision33 est une firme mondiale de services de conseils professionnels en informatique qui résout les défis commerciaux des clients grâce à des services de conseils transformationnels et des solutions technologiques. Nous travaillons en partenariat avec des organisations en croissance dans les secteurs public et privé pour comprendre leur activité et les aider à réaliser leur vision avec la bonne combinaison de stratégie, de conseil et de technologie. Les membres de l’équipe hautement qualifiés et axés sur les résultats de Vision33 fournissent une expertise transparente en matière de processus commerciaux et de technologie dans divers secteurs et groupes de clients. 
 
Vision33 Canada Inc., un partenaire primé de Sage Intacct, a été nommé gagnant du Sage Platinum Club pour l'exercice 2023.  Vision33 est actuellement à la recherche d'un consultant en implantation de Sage Intacct (bilingue) pour rejoindre notre équipe. Si vous recherchez une opportunité passionnante, nous avons un rôle pour vous!  Dans ce rôle pratique, vous serez responsable de : 

Fournir des services de conseil, de la formation et de l'assistance pour assurer aux clients des déploiements réussis de solutions logicielles de comptabilité en infonuagique. 
Travailler directement avec les directeurs financiers, les directeurs informatiques et les utilisateurs finaux pour façonner des solutions qui accèdent aux informations

title: Cloud IT Consultant
description:
Cloud IT Consultant, Cloud AWS ServicesFull Time / Permanent

Ready to take cloud deployments to the next level? Join our fast-growing team as an AWS Cloud IT Consultant, where you’ll design, deploy, and optimize AWS environments for SAP Business One and SAGE clients.

You’ll work hands-on with Linux and Windows servers, manage web and application services, and support client go-lives—even after hours. Your expertise will help shape scalable, secure cloud systems that drive real business results.

What You’ll Do:

Deploy AWS environments for SAP & SAGE clients.
Configure and support web and application services (IIS, Apache, Nginx) and file/print services.
Lead go-live migrations, including after-hour cutovers. 


Document build processes, adhere to policies, develop and maintain procedural documentation, and support continuous improvement. 


Collaborate with clients, consultants, and stakeholders to ensure successful builds and deployments.


Monitor system and database backups to ensure processes are running as expected.
Complete environmental restorations and server maintenance.
Respond to security events and resolve issues. 
Identify potential issues and drive proactive solutions.


Experience Required for the Role:

5+ years’ AWS or other cloud deployment.
Strong Linux skills (SUSE preferred) and solid Windows/AD/Networking knowledge (VPN, Firewalls, AWS Security groups).
Deployment of Windows Servers (MS SQL, RDP, DC, GTW, WEB, etc.).
Familiarity with SAP HANA and ERP

description:
Customer Relationship Manager, ERP – Eastern U.S.Permanent Full Time Remote/WFH 
Location: Open to Eastern U.S. Locations 

Drive Client Success. Expand Strategic Accounts. Grow Your Earnings.
If you’re an experienced ERP account manager who thrives on building long-term client partnerships and growing strategic accounts, Vision33 offers the platform to take your career further.

In this Customer Relationship Manager (CRM) role, you will work with an established base of SAP Business One clients across the Eastern U.S. geographies, partnering with executive teams to unlock new opportunities for growth, efficiency, and innovation. With strong delivery teams behind you and uncapped earning potential, this is an opportunity to expand both your client impact and your career.

At Vision33, we combine best-in-class cloud technology with deep industry expertise to businesses scale, modernize, and operate more efficiently. Our collaborative sales and delivery teams empower Customer Relationship Managers to focus on what they do best—building trusted partnerships and driving meaningful client outcomes.

If you’re motivated by owning strategic relationships, driving account growth, and consistently increasing your earnings through performance year over year, you’ll thrive in this role.

Location: Canada Open - Remote We’re in the process of building something new at Vision33 — a dedicated S/4HANA Public Cloud team that will play a central role in the future of ERP. Leading the design, configuration, and implementation of S/4HANA Public Cloud supply chain solutions across multiple industries.

title: Customer Experience Manager, Cloud Delivery
location: St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
description:
Cloud Experience Manager 
Full Time/Permanent  
Required Location: St. John’s, NL  (Hybrid: Office and Work-from-Home St. John's, NL)

Make our cloud delivery feel seamless, confident, and controlled.  
  
As Cloud Experience Manager, you will own the end-to-end client experience for cloud service delivery — from quoting and implementation through go-live and support transition. This role is equal parts coordination, leadership, and customer advocacy – including both delivery leadership and operational ownership.

Your focus is ensuring cloud solutions are delivered smoothly, meet commercial and operational expectations, and transition successfully into live production with minimal friction.

Partner with sales to align delivery timelines, scope, and customer expectations.  


Central lead for customer delivery experience, coordinating across sales, engineering, delivery, and operations.

Support clients through training, testing, go-live readiness, and post-go-live adoption to drive fast time to value.

title: Senior Performance Marketing Manager
description:
Senior Performance Marketing Manager
US, Remote
Apply
About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This role owns paid distribution at CoLab. You’ll own the performance, growth, and evolution of our paid media program — with LinkedIn as the primary channel, but not the only one. Your job is to take what’s already working, improve it month over month, and expand into new channels that help us reach the right buyers in new ways.

That means running and scaling LinkedIn paid social, owning our paid newsletter program, and designing thoughtful experiments in emerging or underutilized channels (e.g., Reddit, YouTube, or other

Litigation Paralegal
Litigation Paralegal | Halifax, NS



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

We are seeking a permanent, full-time Litigation Paralegal to join our Halifax office to provide paralegal services to the Litigation Practice Groups.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES

Draft pleadings,
Utilize Litigation Support Software to manage document production, disclosure and organization, including the following tasks:
data entry to objectively code documents
preparation of Affidavits Disclosing Documents (including review and redact for privilege and relevance);
compile Books of Evidence/Exhibits;
document production;
import documents received from opposing counsel;
prepare legal chronologies;
Record all billable and non-billable functions on a daily basis;
Utilize computer technology in an efficient and productive manner;
General office and clerical duties, including billable and non-billable functions;
Other duties as required.
SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

title: Associate Vice-President, Human Resources
description:
The University of Lethbridge invites applications for the position of Associate Vice-President (AVP), Human Resources. This is a significant leadership opportunity to advance people, culture, and organizational effectiveness at a pivotal time for the University. 

The Organization

Founded in 1967, the University of Lethbridge is a comprehensive academic and research institution recognized for its commitment to liberal education, experiential learning, and community engagement. With campuses in Lethbridge and Calgary, the University serves approximately 8,000 students and employs a complex workforce of faculty and staff represented across eight different employee groups. Guided by a new strategic plan and energized by recent academic program expansions, including the launch of a new medical program and the planned development of an engineering program, the University is poised for transformative growth.

The University of Lethbridge’s Blackfoot name is Iniskim, meaning Sacred Buffalo Stone. The University is located in traditional Blackfoot Confederacy territory. We honour the Blackfoot people and their traditional ways of knowing in caring for this land, as well as all Indigenous Peoples who have helped shape and continue to strengthen our University community.

The Role
The Associate Vice-President (AVP), Human Resources, is the University’s senior human resources leader, responsible for advancing a strategic, people-centred, service-oriented HR function that supports the a

title: Interim Executive Director
location: St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, CA
description:
Interim Executive Director
Single Parent’s Association of Newfoundland and Labrador (SPANL)
St. John’s, NL

Step into a role where strong leadership meets real-world impact, guiding an organization that changes lives every day.

The Company

SPANL was founded in September of 1987 by Margaret Acreman. Based on her own experience as a single parent, she recognized the gaps in available services and little awareness of the issues facing single parent families.  Today, the Single Parent Association of Newfoundland and Labrador (SPANL) is a dynamic, non-profit organization dedicated to empowering single parents on their journey to overcome challenges and achieve their goals. Through a comprehensive range of programs and services—including education, emergency assistance, and community supports—SPANL fosters self-sufficiency and resilience in families across the province.

SPANL creates a strong sense of belonging through social initiatives, peer support, and volunteer engagement. With a mission to elevate the quality of life for single parent families while promoting sustainability, collaboration, and inclusivity, SPANL continues to play a vital and respected role in Newfoundland and Labrador’s social fabric.

The Role 

This is a full-time, one-year term position offered as maternity leave coverage.

Reporting to the Board of Directors, the Executive Director is responsible for the overall leadership, operational management, and financial stewardship of SPANL. This includes ensur

title: Vice-President, Finance and Operations
description:
The University of Windsor is seeking an experienced and values-driven leader to serve as its Vice-President, Finance and Operations (VPFO). This current vacancy is a pivotal opportunity to shape the future of the University through strategic, innovative, and collaborative leadership in support of UWindsor’s academic mission and long-term success.

The Organization

The University of Windsor is situated on the traditional territory of the Three Fires Confederacy of First Nations: the Ojibwa, the Odawa, and the Potawatomi.

The University is a comprehensive research and teaching institution with more than 16,000 students. Located in the heart of Canada's Border City, the University of Windsor combines excellence in teaching, research, and professional practice with a commitment to build stronger communities, treat people with respect and dignity, and address pressing societal needs. 

The Role

Reporting to the President and Vice-Chancellor, the Vice-President, Finance and Operations (VPFO) provides strategic, ethical, and people-centred leadership across the University’s administrative and operational portfolios. These include finance, facilities, information technology, ancillary services, environmental sustainability, enterprise risk management, and campus safety.

As a key member of the senior leadership team, the VPFO works in close partnership with the Provost to advance integrated planning and decision-making that aligns resources with academic priorities and long-term i

title: Vice President, Strategic Academic Initiatives
description:
Yorkville University is seeking a strategic, innovative, and collaborative academic leader to serve as its next Vice-President, Strategic Academic Initiatives. This is a unique opportunity to shape and advance transformative academic priorities, institutional partnerships, and future-focused initiatives that strengthen student success and organizational impact.

Yorkville University
At Yorkville University and Toronto Film School, we believe education is more than the pursuit of knowledge – it is a catalyst for transformation. Our mission, grounded in democratizing education, is to create access for learners from every background and empower them to achieve their aspirations and advance their careers.

We are committed to delivering rigorous, flexible programs that are both personally fulfilling and professionally rewarding, while contributing to the betterment of society. Since 2004, Yorkville has been among Canada’s few private, for-profit, fully accredited degree-granting universities, with campuses in Ontario, British Columbia, and New Brunswick. Together with Toronto Film School, we offer creative and academic pathways at both the college and university levels, cultivating a multidisciplinary environment where innovation and the arts converge.

Our community is vibrant and diverse. Many of our learners are adults balancing education with careers and personal commitments. Every member of our team – whether in teaching, operations, or technology – is dedicated to removing

title: Senior Director, Strategic Operations
description:
Job Description

About RBCx

RBCx pursues big, bold ideas and leverages RBC’s extensive experience, networks, and capital to help shape what’s next. Our four pillars – Banking, Capital, Platform, and Portfolio – combine to support tech businesses of all sizes and stages, making us the go-to backer of Canadian innovation. Our people are our most valuable assets, so we invest deeply in ensuring RBCx isn’t just a place to work but a place to belong.

What is the Opportunity?

We are seeking a visionary leader to oversee critical functions at the intersection of strategy, finance, and operations within RBCx. This role will drive growth, operational excellence, and financial stewardship across a diverse portfolio, including high-impact initiatives and large-scale acquisitions. The successful candidate will lead a dynamic team responsible for financial planning, strategic prioritization, and scalable process design, while partnering with senior executives to deliver on RBC’s strategic objectives.

title: Director of Finance and Accounting
location: Saint John, NB, New Brunswick, CA
description: Are you a strategic financial leader with a passion for driving business growth and operational excellence? On behalf of Tru North Capital Inc., we are seeking a Director of Finance and Accounting to lead the financial strategy and operations of this dynamic, fast-growing organization. About Tru North Capital Inc. Tru North Capital Inc. is a results-driven real estate development company committed to innovation, excellence, and long-term success. We value strong leadership, long-term partnerships, collaboration, and proactive financial management that support our ambitious growth strategy. Headquartered in Saint John, New Brunswick, Tru North Capital Inc. operates a diversified portfolio of properties, including Retail, Residential, Commercial development, Construction, and property management, with most business activities based in the region, alongside a portfolio of properties in the United States. Key Responsibilities As the Director of Finance and Accounting, you will play a critical leadership role in shaping the financial future of the organization. Your key responsibilities include: Financial Strategy & Leadership: Develop and implement financial strategies that align with the company’s growth objectives, ensuring long-term financial sustainability. Financial Reporting & Compliance: Oversee the preparation and accuracy of monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements, ensuring compliance with regulatory and accounting standards. title: Senior Power Platform Consultant
description: Venor is proud to partner with IMP Solutions on their search for a Senior Power Platform Consultant to join their team! IMP Solutions, a division of IMP Group Limited, provides IT products, IT services, and business consulting solutions to clients across Canada. As a full-service technology partner, they support clients across the entire technology lifecycle; from strategy and advisory through implementation, delivery, managed services, and ongoing support. Their team works across cloud, infrastructure, cybersecurity, and business transformation to deliver practical and scalable solutions that create meaningful business outcomes. With headquarters in Halifax and more than 25 years of success, IMP Solutions has built a strong reputation for delivering service excellence across industries including government, healthcare, education, aerospace and defense, financial services, communications, manufacturing, hospitality, and more. As part of their growing Microsoft Professional Services team, IMP Solutions is seeking a highly skilled Senior Power Platform Consultant to help organizations modernize business processes through innovative Microsoft solutions. This role will lead the design, development, deployment, and management of enterprise Power Platform solutions while partnering closely with clients to translate complex requirements into scalable, business-driven outcomes. title: SAP Business One Production Consultant, Brutos ERP
description:
SAP Business One Production Consultant, Brutos ERP 
Full Time / Permanent Location:Open Canada – East Coast  Language Requirement: English (Preference for English with minimum conversational French) 
 
Ready to combine your SAP Business One expertise with your passion for craft beverages? 

Brutos by Vision33 is growing, and we’re looking for an experienced SAP Business One (Production-focused) Implementation Consultant to help breweries and distilleries streamline operations with our industry-focused ERP solution powered by SAP Business One. 

Brutos by Vision33 is a beverage ERP software that helps brewery and distillery manufacturers increase operational efficiency and gain 360-degree visibility into their business. It is an all-in-one ERP software that harnesses the power of SAP Business One to centralize data by combining front- and back-office functions.  

If you thrive in customer-facing ERP consulting roles and love solving complex production and manufacturing challenges, this could be your next great opportunity. 

Your blend of responsibilities: 

Lead full-cycle SAP Business One implementations, upgrades, and migrations for brewery and distillery customers.  


Guide clients through business process improvements and identify system gaps and optimization opportunities.  


Build strong customer relationships through ongoing consultation and problem-solving.  


Configure solutions, create documentation and reports, and support successful rollouts.

Electrical Engineering Technologist/Technician

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.




Kraken Robotics is currently recruiting for an Electrical Engineering Technologist/Technician to join our Mount Pearl, NL office.

Build technology that operates where few others can—deep ocean environments.

At Kraken Robotics, we design advanced subsea imaging, battery, and robotic systems used around the world. We’re growing our engineering team and looking for an Electrical Engineering Technologist/Technician to contribute to the design of mission-critical electronics for next-generation ocean technologies.

You’ll report to the Engineering Manager, Newfoundland (10+ years in subsea systems engineering).

Design and development of electrical systems within our Remotely Operated Towed Vehicle (ROTV), Katfish
Designing wiring harnesses and cable assemblies for ROTVs and their associated topside control equipment, considering factors such as signal integrity, thermal management, and EMC/EMI compliance.

title: Pension Assistant
description: NSHEPP is one of the largest registered pension plans in Nova Scotia, serving over 40,000 active members and 17,000 retirees, with over 90 participating employers at 150+ sites throughout Nova Scotia. On behalf of NSHEPP, we are recruiting a Pension Assistant to provide administrative support for Pension Operations. Reporting to the Manager, Pension Administration, the Pension Assistant will provide superior customer service to Plan members by providing accurate and timely information regarding their pension benefits, answering general inquires (telephone or e-mail) and ensuring all documentation for the members’ benefit entitlements are completed accurately. The Pension Assistant will provide office administrative support to the Pension Services team, will support all aspects of preparing documentation related to pension benefits entitlements and will support and assist staff in successfully achieving pension plan goals and initiatives. The ideal candidate will have completed post-secondary education in a business or administration-related discipline. The successful candidate will have advanced proficiency in Outlook, Word and Excel and will be able to learn new software and systems quickly. The Pension Assistant is someone who possesses appropriate sound judgement, has the behavior required to work in a fast-paced, high-volume, demanding environment and has excellent organizational, problem solving, analytical, multitasking and time-management skills. title: Sales Consultant
description: Venor is proud to partner with Bath Fitter in their search for a Sales Consultant to join their team in Dartmouth, NS. This is an opportunity for a confident, self-directed sales professional who excels at building immediate trust, identifying customer needs, and closing business through a consultative, results-driven approach. Bath Fitter is a recognized leader in home improvement, known for its customer-first culture, operational excellence, and commitment to performance-driven growth. For more than 40 years, Bath Fitter has helped homeowners transform their bathrooms through innovative products, professional installation, and an exceptional customer experience. The company invests heavily in training, leadership development, and proven sales systems that enable team members to build rewarding and successful careers. The Sales Consultant plays a critical role in helping homeowners bring their renovation goals to life. Acting as a trusted advisor throughout the sales process, this individual will build relationships, understand customer needs, present tailored solutions, and guide clients through the decision-making process while delivering an outstanding customer experience. What You’ll Be Doing In this role, you will combine consultative selling, product expertise, and exceptional customer service to drive sales growth and customer satisfaction. Key Responsibilities: Meet with prospective customers to understand their renovation needs and recommend Bath Fitter solutions. C

title: Dock Sales & Service Specialist
description: Venor is proud to partner with Great Canadian Docks in their search for a Dock Sales and Service Specialist to join their Marine Solutions team in Kenora, ON. Great Canadian Docks is a leader in the marine industry, providing premium-quality waterfront solutions through its direct-to-market locations and a strong network of independent dealers spanning from Saskatchewan to Newfoundland. As the exclusive Central and Eastern Canadian distributor for EZ Dock, the industry leader in modular floating dock systems for more than 30 years, Great Canadian Docks delivers best-in-class marine solutions serving a broad range of clients from homeowners and marinas to parks, resorts, and government agencies. Great Canadian Docks has earned a strong industry reputation through its comprehensive product offerings, dedication to reliable solutions and exceptional service, and a focus on helping clients protect and maximize their waterfront investments. About the Role: Reporting to the Director of Sales, the Dock Sales and Service Specialist will play a crucial role in developing strategic partnerships, work with customers to understand their waterfront needs, and help design practical, real-world dock solutions. This role is ideal for someone who is enthusiastic about the marine industry, working with their hands, enjoys being on the water, and building long-term relationships with customers in a cottage-country environment. title: General Manager
description: An Opportunity to Lead, Grow, and Make an Impact Our client is a respected and growing provider of essential support services to the construction, infrastructure, and industrial sectors. With an established reputation for safety, reliability, and customer service, the organization is seeking a dynamic General Manager to lead operations, drive profitability, and support continued growth. This is an opportunity for an entrepreneurial leader who thrives in a fast-paced environment, enjoys building high-performing teams, and has the business acumen to balance strategic growth with operational excellence. Reporting directly to ownership, the General Manager will have broad responsibility for the overall success of the business, including operations, financial performance, business development, and people leadership. The successful candidate will bring energy, accountability, and a hands-on approach to leadership, helping position the organization for its next stage of growth. What You'll Be Doing Operational Leadership Provide overall leadership and direction for daily operations across multiple service divisions. Ensure services are delivered safely, efficiently, and profitably while maintaining high customer satisfaction. title: Industry Strategy Lead
description:
Industry Strategy Lead
NL, Remote
Apply
About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

Our Ideal Candidate

This role is a high-leverage operator reporting into the Director of Industry Strategy & Transformation, with direct exposure to revenue mentorship and account owners. This role exists to inject strategic execution into CoLab’s enterprise deals, sharpen focus to scale wins, translate platform capability into enterprise strategy, and ensure we maintain product market fit.

This is a deal-facing, customer-facing, and product-influencing role. In this role, you will drive consensus inside complex manufacturing organizat

Job description

Genesis Venture Manager, CDL Atlantic

At Genesis, we help build amazing companies. We back founders with the support, connections, and expertise they need to grow.

Genesis, in partnership with Creative Destruction Lab (CDL), is seeking a Venture Manager for a one-year maternity leave replacement position. Based at Genesis in St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, this role will support and report to the CDL-Atlantic team (operated out of Dalhousie University). Founded at the University of Toronto's Rotman School of Management, CDL provides startups with access to a powerful network of accomplished entrepreneurs and investors through a recurring nine-month, milestone-based mentoring program focused on developing scalable, science-based startups.

The Genesis Venture Manager will be the CDL representative on the ground in Newfoundland and Labrador. During the execution of the job duties, there will be a focus on deepening the relationships with all stakeholders

title: Senior Corporate Secretary
description:
Headquartered in Bermuda, with long-standing offices in Halifax and Toronto, Allshores Limited is a publicly traded insurance, health, and wealth management organization and holding company listed on the Bermuda Stock Exchange (BSX). Formed through the 2025 amalgamation of BF&M and Argus, Allshores brings together nearly 200 years of combined experience to create a stronger, unified organization serving communities across Bermuda, the Caribbean, Europe, and North America.

As they continue to evolve and grow, Allshores is seeking a Senior Corporate Secretary to play a key role in supporting the effective operation of the Boards of Directors across their group of companies. Serving as a liaison between the Boards, executive leadership team, subsidiary entities, and external stakeholders, you will be responsible for coordinating board and committee activities, maintaining corporate records, supporting regulatory requirements, and facilitating the timely flow of information across multiple jurisdictions. You will also provide leadership and oversight to the Corporate Officer role, ensuring the effective delivery of corporate secretarial and board administration activities across the organization.

This is an exceptional opportunity for an experienced corporate secretary to work closely with senior leadership and Boards of Directors within a complex, multi-jurisdictional organization with an international footprint.

	Bring at least 10 years of corp

title: Senior Policy Advisor
description:
Do you want to be part of a mission driven organization shaping Canada’s innovation future? Do you thrive in collaborative environments where creativity, continuous improvement, and strategic thinking are valued? Are you motivated by work that strengthens Canada’s competitiveness and delivers meaningful impact for communities and businesses?

At Mitacs, we take a talent first approach to building Canada’s innovation capacity. We connect researchers with organizations across every region of the country to help solve pressing challenges and accelerate economic growth. By enabling business academic collaboration and supporting the development of highly skilled talent, we contribute to a resilient, future ready Canadian economy. 
 
Guided by our values — Impact, Integrity, Collaboration, Excellence, and Talent — we work with partners nationwide to advance research, commercialization, and innovation. 
 
At Mitacs, you become part of a team committed to empowering Canadian talent, fostering meaningful partnerships, and driving innovation that supports Canada’s long-term competitiveness. If you’re inspired by big ideas and passionate about creating national impact, we encourage you to apply.
