 Job Posts Donald Hill Family Postdoctoral Fellow – Artificial Intelligence In Health & Medicine

DONALD HILL FAMILY POSTDOCTORAL FELLOW – ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE IN HEALTH & MEDICINE

Dalhousie University invites applications for a Donald Hill Family Postdoctoral Fellowship in Artificial Intelligence in Health & Medicine. This is an exceptional opportunity for a talented and ambitious researcher to build an independent, high-impact research program at the intersection of machine learning and healthcare.

The fellowship is anchored in the Faculty of Computer Science, under the supervision of Dr. Frank Rudzicz, Dalhousie’s Killam Memorial Chair in AI and Canada CIFAR Chair in AI. The co-supervision team will be assembled around the candidate’s own research vision, drawing from partners across Engineering, Medicine, Health, and Science.

Research Scope
The fellowship is open-ended in research direction, within the area of machine learning applied to health and medicine. We are interested in candidates with expertise and ambition in one or more of the following areas — though we welcome compelling proposals that go beyond them:

Foundation models and large language models in clinical settings (EHR, clinical notes, patient communication)
Speech and language biomarkers for neurological and cognitive disease (dementia, Parkinson’s, depression, psychosis)

ANALYST, DATA SOURCING

WHAT IS THE OPPORTUNITY?
The SOR/APP declaration process as laid out in the data sourcing guideline is a structured and repeatable approach designed to efficiently identify and manage Systems of Record (SORs) and Authorized Provisioning Points (APPs) in accordance with the Enterprise Information Management Risk Policy

The Analyst, Data Sourcing is responsible for assisting with the SOR/APP declaration process to identify and declare SORs/APPs for fully managed domains and providing high level advisory support to self-managed domains to assist them in completing the end-to-end SOR/APP declaration process.

Role will maintain relationships with various business & IT stakeholders to understand & communicate details of the SOR/APP declaration process

WHAT WILL YOU DO

Assisting SOR/APP Declaration Process:

Analyze data lineage to identify and validate Systems of Record (SOR) and Authorized Point of Publication (APP) for Critical Data Elements (CDEs).
Collaborate with lineage analysts, stakeholders, and committees to review, present, and finalize declarations.
Document and maintain evidence of approvals in Collibra and SharePoint, ensuring compliance with RBC standards.

Guide domains through SOR/APP self-management, including enablement training and process clarification.

MICROSOFT FABRIC DATA ENGINEERING SPECIALIST

Position Description:
CGI is seeking a Microsoft Fabric Data Engineer Specialist to support enterprise data modernization initiatives across public sector and commercial clients.

In this role, you will design and implement modern data engineering platforms using Microsoft Fabric, supporting organizations transitioning from traditional Microsoft data platforms to scalable cloud-based analytics architectures.

The successful candidate will have strong hands-on data engineering experience, consulting delivery experience, and the ability to work directly with client stakeholders to deliver production-grade data platforms.

This role is ideal for a senior data engineer with experience modernizing legacy Microsoft data environments such as SQL Server and SSIS into modern Lakehouse architectures.

Data Engineering & Platform Development
Design and implement enterprise data platforms using Microsoft Fabric
Develop Lakehouse architectures using One Lake, Delta tables, and Spark
Build scalable data ingestion and transformation pipelines using Fabric Data Factory
Implement data transformations using PySpark and SQL
Design medallion architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold) for enterprise analytics environments
Optimize pipelines for performance, scalability, and cost

title: Senior Platform Engineer
description: Venor is proud to partner with QuickFacts on their search for a Senior Platform Engineer to join their growing team! QuickFacts is a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) company transforming how insurance professionals access and manage information. Their platform brings together underwriting guidelines, carrier documentation, workflows, training resources, and comparison tools into a single, searchable experience that saves clients valuable time and improves decision-making. As QuickFacts continues to expand its AI-powered platform, they are seeking a seasoned backend engineer who thrives on building scalable systems, solving complex technical challenges, and developing innovative solutions that deliver meaningful business impact. This is an opportunity to join a growing technology company where your work will directly influence product strategy, platform architecture, and the future of AI-driven information management within the insurance industry. The Opportunity As a Senior Platform Engineer, you will play a critical role in designing and scaling the services, infrastructure, APIs, and data systems that power QuickFacts' next generation of products. This position sits at the intersection of software engineering, large-scale information systems, and applied AI. You'll work closely with Product, Front-End Engineering, and DevOps teams to transform complex insurance data into intelligent customer experiences. From designing scalable microservices and data pipelines to optimizing AI-

title: Director of Admissions
description: Title: Director of Admissions  Location: St. John’s, NL  Drive enrollment growth, conversion performance, and student success.  Venor is proud to partner with our client, Keyin College in their search for a results-driven Director of Admissions to lead student recruitment and enrollment across our growing portfolio of programs.  This senior leadership role will drive admissions performance, enrollment growth, and conversion outcomes through strong team leadership, pipeline management, and operational execution.  About Keyin College  Keyin College is one of Newfoundland and Labrador’s leading post-secondary institutions, with more than 40,000 graduates since 1980. As the province’s largest private college, we provide career-focused education designed to equip learners with practical, in-demand skills that align with workforce needs.  As we continue to grow nationally, we are evolving our admissions function to operate with greater sales rigor, accountability, forecasting discipline, and conversion focus — ensuring we attract, engage, and enroll the right students at scale.  The Role  As Director of Admissions, you will oversee the full admissions funnel, from inquiry to enrollment, ensuring the team consistently delivers strong conversion results and an exceptional prospective student experience.  You will lead a high-performing admissions team in a fast-paced, target-driven environment, fostering a culture of accountability, coaching, responsiveness, and continuous improvemen

title: Logistics Coordinator
description: Venor is proud to partner with our client, Tamal Industrial Rubber Ltd., in the recruitment of a Logistics Coordinator for their Mount Pearl warehouse.     About Tamal Industrial Rubber Ltd.  Tamal Industrial Rubber Ltd. is Atlantic Canada’s leading industrial rubber supplier, which prides itself on playing a foundational role in the region’s heaviest sectors. We have a dedicated team of experts operating right here in Newfoundland and Labrador. A committed team that is proud to supply robust, high-quality components for a demanding industrial market.  The Opportunity  The Logistics Coordinator will manage the facility's daily inbound and outbound logistics, inventory tracking, and warehouse organization. In this role, you will ensure absolute accuracy and safety during the receiving, storing, packing, and shipping of all goods while maintaining a highly organized and compliant workspace.  Key Responsibilities  Oversee the unloading of goods from trucks. Unpack, inspect, and verify incoming shipments against invoices or purchase orders.  Document any shortages or discrepancies and reject damaged items. Determine the best shipping methods and arrange transport.  Assemble containers/crates, pack, seal, and label outgoing products with precise identifying information and shipping instructions. Prepare and manage bills of lading, customs forms, invoices, and other logistical documents (manually or via computer).  Maintain internal, accurate digital or manual record-keeping system

title: General Manager
description: Venor is proud to partner with Nurse Next Door in their search for a General Manager to lead and grow their Nova Scotia operations. Nurse Next Door is a global home care agency focused on helping people live happier, healthier lives at home and within the communities they call their own. Founded in Vancouver, Nurse Next Door operates through over 400 locally owned and operated locations across Canada, the USA, Australia, and the UK. As one of North America's leading home care providers, Nurse Next Door is redefining how care is delivered by helping seniors remain independent, engaged, and comfortable in their own homes. Built on a philosophy of compassion, innovation, and exceptional customer experiences, the organization has earned a reputation for delivering care that goes beyond tasks and truly makes a difference in people's lives. As General Manager, you will be responsible for the overall success of the Halifax franchise, leading revenue growth, profitability, business development, operations, and team performance. This role combines the responsibilities of a business leader, community ambassador, and people leader, requiring someone who can identify growth opportunities, build strategic partnerships, develop high-performing teams, and deliver exceptional client experiences. Reporting to the Franchise Owner, you will have the opportunity to make a meaningful impact on both business performance and the lives of seniors and their families throughout Nova Scotia.  What You'

title: Warehousing Manager
description: Venor is proud to partner with our client, Tamal Industrial Rubber Ltd., in the recruitment of a Warehousing Manager for their Mount Pearl warehouse.     About Tamal Industrial Rubber Ltd.  Tamal Industrial Rubber Ltd. is Atlantic Canada’s leading industrial rubber supplier, which prides itself on playing a foundational role in the region’s heaviest sectors. We have a dedicated team of experts operating right here in Newfoundland and Labrador. A committed team that is proud to supply robust, high-quality components for a demanding industrial market.  The Opportunity  The Warehousing Manager will oversee daily logistics operations, coordinate on-the-floor staff, and ensure absolute inventory accuracy across our facility. In this role, you will bridge the gap between upper management and daily operations to ensure the safe, timely, and cost-effective receiving, storing, shipping, and distribution of all materials, parts, and products.  Key Responsibilities  Directing shift schedules, assigning daily tasks, training new hires, and conducting performance reviews.  Overseeing stock takes, tracking shipments, and resolving inventory discrepancies to prevent shortages or overstocking.  Enforcing workplace safety regulations, conducting equipment inspections, and ensuring adherence to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).  Identifying inefficiencies in workflows and implementing process improvements to maximize productivity.  Collaborating with procurement, sales, and logistics teams

SUMMARY OF DUTIES

Introduction:  


The Embassy of Canada in Paris, France is pleased to announce a staffing process for an Export Development Canada (EDC) Associate Regional Manager (ARM) position at the LE-O1 level within the Embassy of Canada in Paris.

Export Development Canada (EDC) provides financing, working capital, risks mitigation solutions and market knowledge to help Canadian exporters and investors expand their international business. EDC helps 8,400 Canadian companies and their global customers in up to 200 markets worldwide each year.

General Manager

Higher Talent is excited to conduct a search for a dynamic, forward thinking General Manager for The City of Corner Brook’s Civic Centre.

WHO WE ARE:

Corner Brook, the garden city and hub of enriched history is located on the western shores of Newfoundland, at the mouth of the Bay of Islands. With a spectacular combination of ocean and mountain scenery, four seasons, and friendly people, Corner Brook is known around the world as both a popular tourist attraction and one of the best places to live, work, and do business. There is plenty of local shopping and restaurants in the city. Corner Brook is also known for its outdoor facilities, such as Marble Mountain Ski Resort. All this in the friendliest climate in Canada, surrounded by some of the most beautiful wilderness in the world.

To learn more about us, please visit The City of Corner Brook.

THE ROLE:

The General Manager of the Civic Centre is a dynamic, forward-thinking and strategic leader responsible for the overall management and operation of the City’s premier multi-purpose facility. This role oversees all aspects of the Civic Centre’s business functions, including sales, marketing, food and beverage services, public relations, finance, human resources, box office operations, ice and facility booking, event services, maintenance, housekeeping, security, and safety. title: Vice-President, Strategic Growth, Productivity & Innovation
description:
Humber Polytechnic is seeking a visionary leader to serve as their Vice-President, Strategic Growth, Productivity & Innovation (VP, SGPI).

This is a rare opportunity to shape the future of a leading Canadian polytechnic while influencing the evolving role of post-secondary education in driving economic growth, productivity, and social impact. Operating at the centre of Humber Polytechnic’s strategic agenda, the VP, SGPI will be uniquely positioned to design and lead an integrated growth model that brings together education, industry, government, and global partners in new and transformative ways.

At a time of profound disruption across higher education, driven by changing learner expectations, technological advancement, and shifting economic conditions, this role offers the chance to build something fundamentally new. The VP will architect Humber’s next-generation growth engine, redefining how institutions expand access, diversify revenue, and deliver lifelong learning at scale. Central to this mandate is the ability to drive measurable growth in revenue through the development of new market opportunities, innovative partnership models, and scalable education and service offerings, including those delivered through complex domestic and international collaborations and contractual arrangements. Through this work, the VP will extend Humber’s reach and impact while positioning the institution as a national leader in productivity and a global innovator in polytechnic education.

title: Chief Administrative Officer
description:
Located along the scenic Saint John River in southern New Brunswick, the Town of Grand Bay-Westfield is a growing and vibrant community recognized for its strong quality of life, natural beauty, and close-knit, family-oriented character. Situated just outside the City of Saint John, the Town offers its 6,000 residents and businesses the benefits of a welcoming small-town environment along with strong regional connectivity and access to urban amenities. Formed through the amalgamation of the former communities of Grand Bay and Westfield, the Town has evolved into a progressive municipality focused on sustainable growth, responsible governance, and community vitality.

The Town of Grand Bay – Westfield is seeking a Chief Administrative Officer (CAO). This position serves as the senior administrative leader of the Town and is accountable for the effective and efficient management of all municipal operations. The CAO provides objective and strategic advice to Council and ensures the implementation of policies, priorities, and programs in alignment with legislative and governance frameworks as well as the strategic plan.

Overseeing all municipal departments, the CAO leads a collaborative and well-established leadership team. The organization is characterized by a strong organizational structure, sound governance, a corporate culture that is well appreciated and has an appetite for growth. At a time of emerging opportunity, including anticipated investments and construction of hou

Job Description

RBCx pursues big, bold ideas and leverages RBC’s extensive experience, networks, and capital to help shape what’s next. Our four pillars – Banking, Capital, Platform, and Ventures – combine to support tech businesses of all sizes and stages, making us the go-to backer of Canadian innovation. Our people are our most valuable assets, so we invest deeply in ensuring RBCx isn’t just a place to work but a place to belong.

About Dr.Bill

Dr.Bill is the largest dedicated medical billing provider in Canada, serving 11,000+ physicians and healthcare professionals in Ontario, British Columbia and Alberta. Fully owned by RBC, Dr.Bill is part of the RBCx portfolio. That makes working at Dr.Bill the best of both worlds: our people enjoy the benefits of working for a big company plus the speed and energy of working for a scaling venture.

Dr.Bill doesn’t exist just to facilitate faster, more accurate medical billing for doctors (although we do that very well indeed!). Our greater goal is

title: Remote Physical Therapist - Contract
description:
Job Title: Remote Physical TherapistClassification: 1099 Independent ContractorWork Structure: Fully RemoteLocation: United States (AZ, CO, MO, OH, VA, DC, NY, NC, TN, NJ preferred)License Requirements: Active PT license in a PT Compact member state

About BreatheSuite
BreatheSuite delivers virtual pulmonary rehabilitation to patients with chronic lung conditions across the country. Our mission is to make quality pulmonary rehabilitation accessible, empowering patients to take control of their respiratory health from the comfort of their own homes. We use a multidisciplinary model — PTs, respiratory therapists, and nurse practitioners — supported by a remote therapeutic monitoring platform built specifically for this population.

The Role
We're looking for experienced Physical Therapists to join our remote care team as 1099 contractors. You'll be working with a medically complex population — primarily COPD, ILD, and other chronic lung conditions — conducting functional assessments and developing individualized strategies to help patients move better through breathing strategies, activity pacing, strengthening, and cardiovascular exercise. Pulmonary-specific experience is not required. Our compensated onboarding program covers the pathophysiology, symptom management principles, and treatment strategies you need to work confidently with this population before you see your first patient.

What You'll Do

Conduct telehealth evaluations and develop individualized treatment plans
